rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH MACRI VS. WELLS WHITNEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 27, 2009, line 1, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH MACRI Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint. Off Calendar, as moot. On July 21, 2009, the parties settled. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND VS. ONVOI BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 27, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT SELECT PERSONNEL SERVICES, INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. As pled, the sixth cause of action for fraud is outside of the three-year statute of limitations and is time-barred. The November 18, 2003 letter sent by Regan establishes that plaintiff knew of Select's involvement in the alleged fraud. Plaintiff must also plead fraud with specificity as to Select. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN D GILLETT VS. RON TEEGUARDEN ENTERPRISES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 27, 2009, line 3, PLAINTIFF STEPHEN GILLETT Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment. Motion is granted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. HECTOR J. WOLANSKY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief From Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 27, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT HECTOR WOLANSKY, and, LORRAINE THIEBAUD Motion For Relief From Default And Default Judgment. Motion is denied. Defendants fail to demonstrate mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. Defendants were properly served and aware of the lawsuit, and their failure to properly respond is not excusable. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION VS. SEBASTIAN ALIOTO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 27, 2009, line 5, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, and, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication is denied, failure to shift burden of proof. No evidence that defendant responsible for the Fields loan amount. No evidence presented regarding the establishment of the Fields account. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PRINCESS NORMAN VS. KRISTINA ZEBRATSKI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 27, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT KRISTINA ZEBRATSKI, GEORGE CRAIG, and, CRAWLEY WARREN INSURANCE SERVICES, INC., DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued to July 29, 2009 on the Court's own motion. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. TORINA CRAIG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 27, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT TORINA CRAIG DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained with five days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege that the premises are exempt from the just cause provisions of the rent ordinance. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HSBC BANK (USA) INC. VS. THOMAS MAYFIELD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 27, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT LOTTIE TAYLOR DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Is sustained with five days leave to amend. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FAHED SALFITI VS. MONA STEWART et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 27, 2009, line 9, DEFENDANT RAMONA STEWART DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off calendar. Dismissal entire action filed on July 20, 2009. =(302/CWW)
